prep.summary: The EA Leadership Off-Site is a Q2 2026 session drawing on a backlog of unresolved cross-cutting issues — Matt's active consideration of a Staff Engineer move (decision was due 'tomorrow' per the SE Discussion notes), Rebecca Neussel's pending transition to P&C and the coverage gap she leaves behind, and the broader EA value and governance questions raised in the 4/21 and 5/6 leadership meetings. The ARB documentation exercise triggered by Dan Griffiths' question and the Architecture as Code task team (flagged in the Matt SE Discussion notes) remain open threads with no recorded resolution. Staffing fragility is the connective tissue — Matt's potential exit and Rebecca's confirmed transition both leave domains underserved, and no backfill for Rebecca has been approved.
tasks: Review sent emails for cross-cutting themes — surface anything touching ARB formalization, Architecture as Code progress, or MI engagement that leadership hasn't seen formally.
Prepare a clear status on Matt's Staff Engineer decision — confirm whether he pursued the role, and assess the downstream impact on the Architecture as Code task team if he moved.

Bring a concrete coverage proposal for the gap Rebecca's transition creates — specifically who absorbs CRM/Salesforce and Data & Analytics, and whether the centralization lever for D&A has been acted on.
Document the products and services work in flight — pull together roadmap or TD pipeline items that need leadership visibility, particularly anything stalled in Jeff's approval backlog.
Be ready to address EA value messaging directly — Dan Griffiths' federated-vs-centralized concern from the 4/21 meeting is unresolved and likely to resurface in an off-site leadership context.
prep.title: (Q2) 2026 EA Leadership Off-Site

prep.summary: Your work with this group centers on Gen AI adoption across the P&C architecture function — specifically using AI to automate functional requirement generation, streamline design and requirements gathering, and reduce cycle time in intake and approval processes. The 5/15 TD sync established a concrete direction: lower entry criteria for plan approval to scope and high-level business capabilities, then use AI tools to flush out full requirements — eliminating the need for fully documented requirements upfront. The 5/11 EMG standup flagged capturing 'executable requirements' as one of the organization's three big AI priorities, signaling leadership alignment with the work you've already been driving.
tasks: Review sent emails from the past two to three weeks and pull every concrete reference to automated functional requirement generation and AI-assisted design or requirements gathering — bring specifics, not summaries.
Prepare a concise status on where the AI-driven requirements work stands today — what's been piloted, what's produced output, and what's still in progress.
Identify any open decisions or blockers from the 5/15 TD sync thread — particularly the lowered entry criteria model and whether that workflow change has been formally socialized or documented.
Be ready to connect your work to the 'executable requirements' priority named in the 5/11 standup — frame what you've been doing in those terms for alignment with leadership's current language.
Clarify your ask or desired outcome for this meeting — whether you need a decision, a green light to expand the effort, or simply shared visibility — and lead with that.
prep.title: Gen AI EMG chat & alignment

events.summary: Align on TWT Security Issue - IT Recommendations

prep.summary: The two-way SMS security thread has been active since at least late April — centered on a core tension between the business push for Hi Marley and InfoSec's hard-line requirements: no sensitive data over insecure channels, mandatory MFA for all parties, and exposure risk even from using a member's name in a text. The new CISO (Dan) is driving a stricter posture, a 2025 NYDFS ruling is adding regulatory pressure, and as of the 5/6 sync, the business side (Sarah and Laura) was still reviewing InfoSec's write-up while Raj was tasked with coordinating the next stakeholder meeting. A recurring structural problem — business mandates short-circuiting technical due diligence, with DLP and perimeter security teams arriving late to the conversation — remains unresolved.
tasks: Review InfoSec's formal write-up on two-way SMS requirements — specifically the MFA mandate and the prohibition on sensitive data over unencrypted channels — so you can speak to what the business is being asked to comply with.
Confirm whether Sarah, Laura, and Raj have landed on a path forward since the 5/6 sync, and surface any outstanding blockers before the meeting starts.
Prepare to articulate the NYDFS 2025 ruling and its implications — know how it may set the compliance floor for the industry and how it affects the Hi Marley implementation specifically.
Be ready to address the CCOM retirement risk — the business plans to replace a secure, authenticated channel with Hi Marley SMS, and that tradeoff needs a clear position from IT.
Identify which IT recommendations are on the table for today and whether any require a decision versus further review — come in knowing what you need the room to commit to.
prep.title: Align on TWT Security Issue - IT Recommendations

focus_html:
Focus · Next up
4:00 PM — 4:30 PM

Gen AI EMG chat & alignment

Prep

Your work with this group centers on Gen AI adoption across the P&C architecture function — specifically using AI to automate functional requirement generation, streamline design and requirements gathering, and reduce cycle time in intake and approval processes. The 5/15 TD sync established a concrete direction: lower entry criteria for plan approval to scope and high-level business capabilities, then use AI tools to flush out full requirements — eliminating the need for fully documented requirements upfront. The 5/11 EMG standup flagged capturing 'executable requirements' as one of the organization's three big AI priorities, signaling leadership alignment with the work you've already been driving.

  • Review sent emails from the past two to three weeks and pull every concrete reference to automated functional requirement generation and AI-assisted design or requirements gathering — bring specifics, not summaries.
  • Prepare a concise status on where the AI-driven requirements work stands today — what's been piloted, what's produced output, and what's still in progress.
  • Identify any open decisions or blockers from the 5/15 TD sync thread — particularly the lowered entry criteria model and whether that workflow change has been formally socialized or documented.
  • Be ready to connect your work to the 'executable requirements' priority named in the 5/11 standup — frame what you've been doing in those terms for alignment with leadership's current language.
  • Clarify your ask or desired outcome for this meeting — whether you need a decision, a green light to expand the effort, or simply shared visibility — and lead with that.
